notes.anchor: there was always something, Silius, Drusus, this and that
notes.note: Cicero lists the gardens-owners --- Silius and Drusus --- as the running topics that once gave him daily reason to write; the negotiations over their properties are the business of the shrine-hunt. Now, with that business stalled, he has little to send.

The reappearance of Otho, connected with the Scapula gardens, briefly revived the search for a site for Tullia's shrine. Cicero notes the matter has again been postponed, leaving him nothing fresh to report.
